content.content:
? sx-wisp-wisp-2ypke [EPIC] · mol-witness-patrol   [● P2 · HOOKED]
Assignee: skogix/witness · Type: epic
Created: 2026-03-01 · Updated: 2026-03-01

DESCRIPTION
Per-rig worker monitor patrol loop.

The Witness is the Pit Boss for your rig. You watch polecats, nudge them toward
completion, verify clean git state before kills, and escalate stuck workers.

**You do NOT do implementation work.** Your job is oversight, not coding.

## Persistent Polecat Model (gt-4ac)

Polecats persist after work completion — sandbox is preserved for reuse:

```
Polecat lifecycle: spawning → working → mr_submitted → idle (sandbox preserved)
MR lifecycle:      created → queued → processed → merged (Refinery handles)
```

Once a polecat calls gt done and submits an MR, it transitions to idle state.
The MR lifecycle continues independently in the Refinery. The polecat is NOT
nuked — its sandbox is preserved for reuse by future slings.

**CRITICAL**: Do NOT nuke polecats with pending MRs. The refinery needs the
remote branch to exist to process the merge. Nuking deletes the remote branch
and orphans the MR. See gt-6a9d.

**Key principle**: Polecat lifecycle is separate from MR lifecycle. Polecats
go idle after work, they are NOT destroyed.

## Design Philosophy

This patrol follows Gas Town principles:
- **Discovery over tracking**: Observe reality each cycle, with minimal agent-bead state for duration tracking
- **Events over state**: POLECAT_DONE mail triggers merge flow registration
- **Persistent by default**: Clean polecats go idle, sandbox preserved for reuse (gt-4ac)
- **Cleanup wisps for merge tracking**: Created when MR is pending in refinery
- **Task tool for parallelism**: Subagents inspect polecats, not molecule arms

## Patrol Shape (Linear)

```
inbox-check ─► process-cleanups ─► check-refinery ─► survey-workers
                                                            │
         ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
         ▼
  check-timer-gates ─► check-swarm ─► patrol-cleanup ─► context-check ─► loop-or-exit
```

No dynamic arms. No fanout gates. No persistent nudge counters.
State is discovered each cycle from reality (tmux, beads, mail).

CHILDREN
  ↳ ○ sx-wisp-wisp-6ga1n: Check own context limit ● P2
  ↳ ○ sx-wisp-wisp-6rpak: Check refinery and deacon health ● P2
  ↳ ○ sx-wisp-wisp-88jwl: End-of-cycle inbox hygiene ● P2
  ↳ ○ sx-wisp-wisp-a1k5b: Process witness mail ● P2
  ↳ ○ sx-wisp-wisp-d11rl: Check if active swarm is complete ● P2
  ↳ ○ sx-wisp-wisp-mhvnl: Check timer gates for expiration ● P2
  ↳ ○ sx-wisp-wisp-r42ze: Inspect all active polecats ● P2
  ↳ ○ sx-wisp-wisp-uca8p: Process pending cleanup wisps ● P2
  ↳ ○ sx-wisp-wisp-z9eek: Loop or exit for respawn ● P2

Gas Town (gt) manages multi-agent workspaces called rigs.

It coordinates agent spawning, work distribution, and communication
across distributed teams of AI agents working on shared codebases.

Usage:
  gt [command]

Work Management:
  bead           Bead management utilities
  cat            Display bead content
  cleanup        Clean up orphaned Claude processes
  close          Close one or more beads
  commit         Git commit with automatic agent identity
  compact        Compact expired wisps (TTL-based cleanup)
  convoy         Track batches of work across rigs
  done           Signal work ready for merge queue
  formula        Manage workflow formulas
  handoff        Hand off to a fresh session, work continues from hook
  hook           Show or attach work on a hook
  mol            Agent molecule workflow commands
  mq             Merge queue operations
  orphans        Find lost polecat work
  prune-branches Remove stale local polecat tracking branches
  ready          Show work ready across town
  release        Release stuck in_progress issues back to pending
  resume         Check for handoff messages
  scheduler      Manage dispatch scheduler
  show           Show details of a bead
  sling          Assign work to an agent (THE unified work dispatch command)
  synthesis      Manage convoy synthesis steps
  trail          Show recent agent activity
  unsling        Remove work from an agent's hook
  wl             Wasteland federation commands

Agent Management:
  agents         List Gas Town agent sessions
  boot           Manage Boot (Deacon watchdog)
  callbacks      Handle agent callbacks
  deacon         Manage the Deacon (town-level watchdog)
  dog            Manage dogs (cross-rig infrastructure workers)
  mayor          Manage the Mayor (Chief of Staff for cross-rig coordination)
  polecat        Manage polecats (persistent identity, ephemeral sessions)
  refinery       Manage the Refinery (merge queue processor)
  role           Show or manage agent role
  session        Manage polecat sessions
  signal         Claude Code hook signal handlers
  witness        Manage the Witness (per-rig polecat health monitor)

Communication:
  broadcast      Send a nudge message to all workers
  dnd            Toggle Do Not Disturb mode for notifications
  escalate       Escalation system for critical issues
  mail           Agent messaging system
  notify         Set notification level
  nudge          Send a synchronous message to any Gas Town worker
  peek           View recent output from a polecat or crew session

Services:
  daemon         Manage the Gas Town daemon
  dolt           Manage the Dolt SQL server
  down           Stop all Gas Town services
  quota          Manage account quota rotation
  shutdown       Shutdown Gas Town with cleanup
  start          Start Gas Town or a crew workspace
  up             Bring up all Gas Town services

Workspace:
  crew           Manage crew workers (persistent workspaces for humans)
  git-init       Initialize git repository for a Gas Town HQ
  init           Initialize current directory as a Gas Town rig
  install        Create a new Gas Town HQ (workspace)
  namepool       Manage polecat name pools
  rig            Manage rigs in the workspace
  worktree       Create worktree in another rig for cross-rig work

Configuration:
  account        Manage Claude Code accounts
  completion     Generate the autocompletion script for the specified shell
  config         Manage Gas Town configuration
  disable        Disable Gas Town system-wide
  enable         Enable Gas Town system-wide
  hooks          Centralized hook management for Gas Town
  issue          Manage current issue for status line display
  plugin         Plugin management
  shell          Manage shell integration
  theme          View or set tmux theme for the current rig
  uninstall      Remove Gas Town from the system

Diagnostics:
  activity       Emit and view activity events
  audit          Query work history by actor
  checkpoint     Manage session checkpoints for crash recovery
  costs          Show costs for running Claude sessions
  dashboard      Start the convoy tracking web dashboard
  doctor         Run health checks on the workspace
  feed           Show real-time activity feed of gt events
  help           Help about any command
  info           Show Gas Town information and what's new
  log            View town activity log
  metrics        Show command usage statistics
  patrol         Patrol digest management
  prime          Output role context for current directory
  seance         Talk to your predecessor sessions
  stale          Check if the gt binary is stale
  status         Show overall town status
  thanks         Thank the human contributors to Gas Town
  version        Print version information
  whoami         Show current identity for mail commands

Additional Commands:
  cycle          Cycle between sessions in the same group
  krc            Key Record Chronicle - manage ephemeral data TTLs
  tap            Claude Code hook handlers
  town           Town-level operations
  warrant        Manage death warrants for stuck agents

Flags:
  -h, --help      help for gt
  -v, --version   version for gt

Use "gt [command] --help" for more information about a command.

Manage the Witness - the per-rig polecat health monitor.

The Witness patrols a single rig, watching over its polecats:
  - Detects stalled polecats (crashed or stuck mid-work)
  - Nudges unresponsive sessions back to life
  - Cleans up zombie polecats (finished but failed to exit)

Polecats manage their own sessions (via gt handoff). The Witness handles
failures and edge cases only.

content.content:
Key Record Chronicle (KRC) manages TTL-based lifecycle for Level 0 ephemeral data.

Per DOLT-STORAGE-DESIGN-V3.md, Level 0 includes patrol heartbeats, status checks,
and other operational data that decays in forensic value over days.

KRC provides:
  - Configurable TTLs per event type
  - Auto-pruning of expired events
  - Statistics on ephemeral data lifecycle

Examples:
  gt krc stats              # Show event statistics
  gt krc prune              # Remove expired events
  gt krc prune --dry-run    # Preview what would be pruned
  gt krc config             # Show TTL configuration
  gt krc config set patrol_* 12h   # Set TTL for patrol events

Usage:
  gt krc [flags]
  gt krc [command]

Available Commands:
  auto-prune-status Show auto-prune scheduling state
  config            View or modify TTL configuration
  decay             Show forensic value decay report
  prune             Remove expired events
  stats             Show statistics about ephemeral data

Flags:
  -h, --help   help for krc

Use "gt krc [command] --help" for more information about a command.

○ sx-wisp-wisp-uca8p · Process pending cleanup wisps   [● P2 · OPEN]
Type: task
Created: 2026-03-01 · Updated: 2026-03-01

DESCRIPTION
Process cleanup wisps (merge tracking and dirty state handling).

Cleanup wisps are created for two reasons:
1. Pending MR: HandlePolecatDone creates a wisp in 'merge-requested' state
   when a polecat has work in the refinery queue. These are resolved when
   the MERGED signal arrives.
2. Dirty state: When a polecat has uncommitted changes or unpushed commits
   that need manual intervention.

```bash
# Find all cleanup wisps
bd list --label cleanup --status=open
```

If no wisps, skip this step (most common case in ephemeral model).

For each cleanup wisp, investigate and resolve the dirty state:

## State: pending (needs investigation)

1. **Extract polecat name** from wisp title/labels

2. **Diagnose the problem**:
```bash
cd polecats/<name>
git status                    # What's uncommitted?
git stash list                # Any stashed work?
git log @{u}..HEAD     # Any unpushed commits?
```

3. **Resolution options**:
   - **Uncommitted changes**: Commit and push, then nuke
   - **Stashed work**: Pop and commit, or discard if not valuable
   - **Unpushed commits**: Push to origin, then nuke
   - **All valuable work lost**: Escalate to Deacon for recovery

4. **If resolvable locally**: Fix and nuke
```bash
# Example: push unpushed commits
git push origin HEAD

# Then nuke
gt polecat nuke <name>

# Close the wisp
bd close <wisp-id> --reason "Resolved: pushed commits, nuked"
```

5. **If needs escalation**: Send RECOVERY_NEEDED to Deacon
```bash
gt mail send deacon/ -s "RECOVERY_NEEDED <rig>/<polecat>" \
  -m "Cleanup Status: <status>
Branch: <branch>
Issue: <issue-id>

Cannot auto-resolve. Please advise."
```
Leave wisp open until Deacon resolves.

## State: merge-requested (legacy, rare)

This state was used before the ephemeral model. If found, the polecat is
waiting for a MERGED signal. The inbox-check step handles these.

**Parallelism**: Use Task tool subagents to process multiple cleanups concurrently.
Each cleanup is independent - perfect for parallel execution.

DEPENDS ON
  → ○ sx-wisp-wisp-a1k5b: Process witness mail ● P2

○ sx-wisp-wisp-mhvnl · Check timer gates for expiration   [● P2 · OPEN]
Type: task
Created: 2026-03-01 · Updated: 2026-03-01

DESCRIPTION
Check for expired timer gates and escalate as needed.

Timer gates are async wait conditions with a timeout. When the timeout expires,
the gate should be escalated to the overseer for human intervention.

**Step 1: Run timer gate check**
```bash
bd gate check --type=timer --escalate
```

This command:
1. Finds all open gate issues with await_type=timer
2. Checks if `now > created_at + timeout`
3. Escalates expired gates via `gt escalate` (HIGH severity)
4. Reports summary of gate status

**Step 2: Review output**

If expired gates were found and escalated:
- The escalation creates an audit trail bead
- Overseer will be notified via mail
- Gate remains open until manually resolved

If no expired gates:
- Continue patrol normally

**Note**: Timer gates do NOT auto-close on expiration. They escalate.
This ensures human oversight of timeout conditions.

**Parallelism**: This is a single command, no parallel execution needed.

content.content:
○ sx-wisp-wisp-z9eek · Loop or exit for respawn   [● P2 · OPEN]
Type: task
Created: 2026-03-01 · Updated: 2026-03-01

DESCRIPTION
End of patrol cycle decision.

**If context LOW** (can continue patrolling):

Resolve your agent bead ID for this patrol cycle. You MUST replace `<YOUR_RIG>` below with your actual rig name (e.g., `beads`, `town`) before running:
```bash
bd list --type=agent --desc-contains="role_type: witness" --json | jq -r '.[] | select(.status != "closed") | select(.description | test("(?m)^\\s*rig: <YOUR_RIG>\\s*$")) | .id'
```
This must return exactly one bead ID. If it returns zero results, STOP and report an error — verify you substituted `<YOUR_RIG>` correctly. If it returns multiple results, STOP and report an error — manual disambiguation is required. Use the single resolved bead ID as YOUR_AGENT_BEAD in the commands below.

Then use await-signal with exponential backoff to wait for activity:

```bash
gt mol step await-signal --agent-bead YOUR_AGENT_BEAD \
  --backoff-base 30s --backoff-mult 2 --backoff-max 5m
```

This command:
1. Subscribes to `bd activity --follow` (beads activity feed)
2. Returns IMMEDIATELY when any beads activity occurs
3. If no activity, times out with exponential backoff:
   - First timeout: 30s
   - Second timeout: 60s
   - Third timeout: 120s
   - ...capped at 5 minutes max
4. Tracks `idle:N` label on your agent bead for backoff state

**On signal received** (activity detected):
Reset the idle counter and start next patrol cycle:
```bash

? sx-wisp-wisp-l11bu [EPIC] · mol-witness-patrol   [● P2 · HOOKED]
Assignee: skogix/witness · Type: epic
Created: 2026-03-01 · Updated: 2026-03-01

DESCRIPTION
Per-rig worker monitor patrol loop.

The Witness is the Pit Boss for your rig. You watch polecats, nudge them toward
completion, verify clean git state before kills, and escalate stuck workers.

**You do NOT do implementation work.** Your job is oversight, not coding.

## Persistent Polecat Model (gt-4ac)

Polecats persist after work completion — sandbox is preserved for reuse:

```
Polecat lifecycle: spawning → working → mr_submitted → idle (sandbox preserved)
MR lifecycle:      created → queued → processed → merged (Refinery handles)
```

Once a polecat calls gt done and submits an MR, it transitions to idle state.
The MR lifecycle continues independently in the Refinery. The polecat is NOT
nuked — its sandbox is preserved for reuse by future slings.

**CRITICAL**: Do NOT nuke polecats with pending MRs. The refinery needs the
remote branch to exist to process the merge. Nuking deletes the remote branch
and orphans the MR. See gt-6a9d.

**Key principle**: Polecat lifecycle is separate from MR lifecycle. Polecats
go idle after work, they are NOT destroyed.

## Design Philosophy

This patrol follows Gas Town principles:
- **Discovery over tracking**: Observe reality each cycle, with minimal agent-bead state for duration tracking
- **Events over state**: POLECAT_DONE mail triggers merge flow registration
- **Persistent by default**: Clean polecats go idle, sandbox preserved for reuse (gt-4ac)
- **Cleanup wisps for merge tracking**: Created when MR is pending in refinery
- **Task tool for parallelism**: Subagents inspect polecats, not molecule arms

## Patrol Shape (Linear)

```
inbox-check ─► process-cleanups ─► check-refinery ─► survey-workers
                                                            │
         ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
         ▼
  check-timer-gates ─► check-swarm ─► patrol-cleanup ─► context-check ─► loop-or-exit
```

No dynamic arms. No fanout gates. No persistent nudge counters.
State is discovered each cycle from reality (tmux, beads, mail).

CHILDREN
  ↳ ○ sx-wisp-wisp-0z5c0: End-of-cycle inbox hygiene ● P2
  ↳ ○ sx-wisp-wisp-7mpac: Check timer gates for expiration ● P2
  ↳ ○ sx-wisp-wisp-ake6m: Inspect all active polecats ● P2
  ↳ ○ sx-wisp-wisp-fxfeq: Loop or exit for respawn ● P2
  ↳ ○ sx-wisp-wisp-shw65: Check refinery and deacon health ● P2
  ↳ ○ sx-wisp-wisp-v0lug: Check if active swarm is complete ● P2
  ↳ ○ sx-wisp-wisp-vzwww: Check own context limit ● P2
  ↳ ○ sx-wisp-wisp-w2roi: Process witness mail ● P2
  ↳ ○ sx-wisp-wisp-xbvu2: Process pending cleanup wisps ● P2
